originalTitle: Yemen’s Houthis claim attacks on Red Sea tankers as US launches 12th night of strikes on Iran – Middle East crisis live
summary: US military says it is degrading Tehran’s ability to threaten ships in strait of Hormuz; Houthis say tankers violated their blockade; US signs nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia
content: Two people have been killed in a US strike at the Shalamcheh border crossing in south-western Iran, the country’s Students News Network is reporting, citing a security offical. A little earlier Iran’s Mehr news agency was cited as quoting a provincial security official saying a US missile attack near a passenger terminal at the Shalamcheh crossing with Iraq had caused no casualties. The reports come as the US continues what Central Command said were more strikes against Iranian military targets, beginning from 5.30pm ET Wednesday (9.30pm GMT). More now on the US signing a landmark nuclear agreement with Saudi Arabia that could allow the kingdom to enrich uranium in the future. Full details of the deal have yet to be released, but the timing of the announcement is controversial as the US’s war with Iran is being fought in part to prevent Tehran enriching uranium domestically and make it near-impossible for Iran to build a nuclear weapon, as Maya Yang and Patrick Wintour report. A civil nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia has been in the works for years during both Donald Trump’s first administration and that of former president Joe ⁠Biden. No deal has come together before now, in part because of warnings from nonproliferation groups that say it could offer Saudi Arabia a path to develop a nuclear weapon. The deal will be submitted to the US Congress for review, where it could face opposition among lawmakers who fear a lack of monitoring safeguards to restrict Saudi Arabia’s uranium enrichment. As mentioned, the US Department of Energy said its secretary and his Saudi counterpart signed the “peaceful nuclear cooperation agreement” along with a “bilateral safeguards agreement”. The full report is here: Before the US launched its 12th night of strikes against Iran, the Islamic republic’s foreign minister warned: “Our defense doctrine is clear: eye for an eye.” Abbas Araghchi also said in the post on X several hours ago: Any aggression against Iran, including our infrastructure, will compel a powerful and decisive response. Those who contribute to such aggression, whatever the kind of support, will also be considered as legitimate targets. More on Yemen’s Houthis claiming strikes on two Saudi oil tankers in the Red Sea: The master of one tanker reported being struck by an unknown projectile about 70 nautical miles south-west of Al Shuqaiq, causing a fire that the crew was fighting, the British naval security monitor UKMTO said. The Iran-aligned Houthis warned shipping companies on Wednesday that their tankers risk being attacked if they used Saudi Arabian ports, as the militia enforced a blockade it announced the previous day. The Houthi warning for the Red Sea – coming on top of Iran’s threat to shipping on the other side of the Arabian peninsular in the Gulf – could affect the substantial Saudi oil exports that have so far been unscathed by the months-long conflict and deepen the rapidly escalating crisis in the region, as Peter Beaumont has written. Donald Trump said he would “take care of” the Houthis if they followed through on their blockade threat against Saudi ports. The Houthis have been broadcasting radio warnings to vessels, the International Chamber of Shipping told Agence France-Presse. The message warned that “in case of non-compliance of Yemeni armed forces decision, those ships will be in our target”. Kuwait’s air defences are responding to hostile drone threats amid “Iranian aggression”, the Kuwaiti army is saying. Any explosion sounds heard were the result of attacks being intercepted, it said on social media. Iran has continued to strike at countries around the region hosting American forces in response to ongoing US attacks. Welcome to our live coverage of the continuing crisis in the Middle East. The US military announced on Thursday evening it had began a 12th consecutive night of strikes against Iranian military targets at the direction of Donald Trump. US Central Command said the strikes would “continue to further degrade Iran’s ability to threaten civilian mariners and commercial vessels transiting regional waters”. It came as Yemen’s Houthis said they carried out missile and drone strikes on Wednesday on two Saudi oil tankers in the Red Sea after declaring a maritime embargo on the kingdom amid the US war with Iran. The Houthis said in a statement posted on Telegram that they “carried out a qualitative military operation targeting two Saudi oil tankers that violated the blockade”, naming the vessels as the Encelia and the Layla. Meanwhile, the US signed a landmark nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia that could allow the kingdom to enrich uranium. The US Department of Energy said on Wednesday its secretary and his Saudi counterpart signed a “peaceful nuclear cooperation agreement”, along with a “bilateral safeguards agreement”. We’ll bring you more on these and other developments in our continuing live coverage.
chineseTitle: 美国对伊朗发动第12晚袭击，也门胡塞武装声称袭击了红海油轮——中东危机直播
chineseSummary: 美国军方表示，这正在削弱德黑兰威胁霍尔木兹海峡船只的能力；胡塞武装称油轮违反了他们的封锁；美国与沙特阿拉伯签署核协议
chineseContent: 伊朗学生新闻网援引一名安全官员的话说，美国对伊朗西南部沙拉姆切边境口岸的袭击造成两人死亡。稍早前，伊朗迈赫尔通讯社援引一名省安全官员的话说，美国在通往伊拉克的沙拉姆切过境点的客运大楼附近发动了导弹袭击，没有造成人员伤亡。这些报道发布之际，美国正按照中央司令部的说法，从周三东部时间下午 5 点 30 分（格林威治标准时间晚上 9 点 30 分）开始，继续对伊朗军事目标进行更多打击。现在更多关于美国与沙特阿拉伯签署一项具有里程碑意义的核协议，该协议可能允许该王国在未来浓缩铀。该协议的全部细节尚未公布，但宣布的时间存在争议，因为美国与伊朗的战争部分是为了阻止德黑兰在国内进行铀浓缩，并使伊朗几乎不可能制造核武器，正如玛雅·杨和帕特里克·温图尔报道的那样。在唐纳德·特朗普第一届政府和前总统乔·拜登执政期间，与沙特阿拉伯的民用核协议已经酝酿多年。此前尚未达成任何协议，部分原因是防扩散组织警告称，该协议可能为沙特阿拉伯提供一条发展核武器的道路。该协议将提交美国国会审查，但可能会遭到立法者的反对，他们担心缺乏限制沙特阿拉伯铀浓缩的监控保障措施。如前所述，美国能源部表示，其部长和沙特外长签署了“和平核合作协议”以及“双边保障协议”。完整报告在这里：在美国对伊朗发动第 12 晚袭击之前，伊斯兰革命组织
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于全球新闻信号，适合用于观察外部环境、政策变化、地缘风险和市场情绪。
